events.title: Philippe Flahaut: Sous le soleil de l’été
events.description: Programme en Fran ç ais pour les enfants de 3 à 12 ans et leurs parents/aidants. Il s’agit d’un programme de chansons traditionnelles de France et du Québec, dont le vocabulaire est axé sur les activités estivales : les vacances, l’eau, le soleil, les animaux et les couleurs. Le programme comprend des chansons à répondre, des chansons accompagnées d’instruments de musique et des jeux musicaux. C’est une expérience interactive qui encourage la participation active des enfants. Aucune inscrip

Buttons made using images of items from the Baldwin Collection of Canadiana. Left: Photograph of Toronto Public Library, Mechanics' Institute building (1890). Right: Map plate from Ortelius' atlas Theatrvm orbis terrarvm (published 1570).

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.description: Qu’est-ce qu’un kaléidoscope ? Une illusion d’optique ? De la magie ? De l’art ? En fait c’est un peu de tout ça. C’est un instrument développé il y a plus de 200 ans qui utilise la réflexion de la lumière sur des miroirs pour créer des motifs répétitifs envoutants. Dans cet atelier, les enfants feront leur propre kaléidoscope et seront exposés aux termes de réflexion et réfraction de lumière tout en étant captivés par la physique des scintillements des motifs sur les surfaces réfléchissantes. events.title: Tinkercad - Pumpkin Design
events.description: In this class you will have an introduction into 3D Design using Tinkercad software.  You will learn how to: alter the size of a sphere, change colour, rotate, copy, group, hollow out a sphere, create a hole and more. This class is for kids aged 8-12.

events.title: Codey Rocky
events.description: Get Ready for an Adventure! Strap in, explorer—it’s time to blast off on your first coding mission with Codey Rocky! In this exciting adventure, you’ll learn how to connect to your robot buddy, send it on its first journey, and use sequencing to guide it’s every move. Whether you're dodging asteroids or exploring new lands, it all starts here. Ready to code your way into action with Codey Rocky? Let’s go! Join us to learn all about the journey of the monarch butterfly. Then use your imagination to design and build your own mock butterfly garden station. Plus, enter a prize draw for a chance to win an awesome book.  For kids ages 6 to 10. Space is limited, please register in advance.

Participants will learn to prepare and use the classic ingredients​ including oat milk and tapioca pearls to make this visually pleasing and sweet drink. events.title: Art Journaling for Teens
events.description: Express yourself visually and connect with your creative side in this art journaling workshop for teens! Art journaling combines traditional journaling with visual art, using sketching, paints, collage, and more. Artist Anna Redish will guide participants with prompts to create unique journal pages. This will be a supportive, judgment-free zone, where participants are encouraged to create their art however feels right to them.

events.title: Introduction to Serger Sewing
events.description: Learn the basics of operating our serger, the Juki MO-735!   In this class we will go over the basic operation and key functions of a serger sewing machine. Threading the machine and safety will be discussed. Between three and five threads are routinely used to sew a seam, trim off the raw edges & finish the edges all at the same time with the Juki MO-735 serger.  However, this workshop focuses solely on learning the 4-thread overlock stitch. They've also learned how to use these tools responsibly, including how to navigate the ethical considerations that come with AI-generated music.

events.title: The A(i), B, C’s of Artificial Intelligence
events.description: This week-long workshop series introduces teens to the four "must knows" of Artificial Intelligence. Through video clips, props, an original AI literacy song, discussions, and hands-on activities, participants will explore how AI works, how it’s used, and how to think critically about its impact. By the end of the program, attendees will be better equipped to navigate AI and social media with awareness and creativity. The workshop is led by Keith McDonald, founder and Chief Curator of Toron

With a mix of comedy, storytelling, and jaw-dropping tricks, this high-energy show turns the audience into Real Magicians by the end! Ages 4+. Drop-in. Large groups must call ahead to attend.

events.title: Beginner's Guide to Making Comics
events.description: Have you ever wanted to create your own comic, manga, or anime-inspired story? In this interactive workshop, teens will learn the basics of comic creation through hands-on activities, creative storytelling, and guided drawing exercises. Participants will explore comic and manga techniques including panels, page layouts, speech bubbles, sound effects, character design, and visual storytelling. They’ll also learn how to build engaging characters and create their own one-page comic or manga scene.

events.title: Toys Alive! Exhibition Tour

events.description: In this workshop led by Coach Desiree McKenzie, participants will explore the global and local roots of spoken word, specifically examining how rap music utilizes poetic tools like rhythm, metaphor, and storytelling. events.title: Income, Expense and Budget
events.description: Understanding how money flows in and out of your household is the foundation of good financial health. Presented by YMCA-GTA, this session, we’ll break down different types of income, common expenses, and how to create a realistic and effective budget. You’ll learn simple, practical tools to track your finances, plan for savings, and avoid common budgeting pitfalls. events.title: Photo Editing with Photoshop
events.description: Adobe Photoshop is the world’s best imaging and graphic design software and is at the core of just about every creative project, from photo editing and compositing to digital painting, animation, and graphic design. Join us for an introductory lesson and learn about the most common tools. We'll explore the workspace and enhance images while investigating tools such as crop, levels, colour balance and more. events.title: Hands-On Dinosaur Fossils & Ancient Life
events.description: Join us for an opportunity to handle, examine up close and discuss nine real and replica fossils in this fascinating hands-on workshop. Participants will be entertained and educated, not just about the fossils themselves, but the paleontological aspects that underpin them. Concepts include: evolution, fossilization, ‘The Tree of Life’ (phylogeny) and — of course — dinosaurs! Presented by Marlowe Andreyko from Dino Bites. This program is for ages 9+ .  Drop-in, no registration required.

events.description: An alternative eco-friendly photography process that uses botanicals as both its subject matter and development process. This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Tuesday to play board games with your friends! Ask us for our list of board games, and check our monthly calendar for Tournaments with prizes! All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Port Union Chess Club
events.description: Looking for a fun after-school activity? Join our Youth Chess Program to learn new skills, play matches, and connect with others!  This program is open to youth ages 7-17 years old. Facilitated by the University of Toronto Scarborough Chess Club. No registration required. Space is limited.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Retirement Planning
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop is an introduction to long-term financial planning including retirement savings and better understanding of federal income benefits, such as, Canada Pension Plan (CPP) and Old Age Security (OAS). Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Cercle de Conversation Française / French Conversation Circle
events.description: Nous vous invitons à pratiquer votre français dans un environnement informel ! Tous les niveaux sont bienvenus. We invite you to practice your French in an informal environment! This French Conversation Circle is open to all levels. Accessibilité: Le sous-titrage en direct ou automatique, l’interprétation en langue des signes, les services d’intervenant, ou les services d’accompagnement sont disponibles sur demande. Veuillez soumettre votre demande au moins deux semaines à l’avance à : tpl.ca/ac

events.title: LivingWorks safeTALK
events.description: LivingWorks safeTALK is a half-day workshop that prepares participants to recognize when someone might be having thoughts of suicide and how to connect them to life-saving support. Learn practical skills to help someone keep safe and guide them toward further help.  Who should attend? Individuals aged 15 and up People who want to support suicide safety in their community No previous training or experience is required. All participants will receive a LivingWorks safeTALK certificat

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have questions about your tablet, phone, laptop, email or an app? Do you need help with eBooks or other online library services? Bring in your own device and our teen volunteers are here to help!

events.title: Knitting Circle
events.description: Join Port Union Library's Knitting Circle. Chat and knit or crochet with a friendly group! You can also share your projects with others!  This is a drop in program but limited to a room capacity of 20 participants. Arrive early to secure your spot.  No registration required.

events.title: Sunday Family Game Time
events.description: Join us for a family games afternoon from Lego to Uno. We will also have some colouring and crafts for everyone Every Sunday afternoon all ages welcome

events.title: Sunday Family Club
events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: D&D for Teens: On-going Campaign
events.description: Play Dungeons and Dragons! Slay monsters and overcome challenges with a Librarian as your Dungeon Master. Ongoing Campaign. This is an in-person program for Teens . Space limited for players*, but watchers are welcome too. All supplies provided. Bring a 1st level character or pick a Pre-generated character. *Register in-person at the Youth Hub at 3:30PM. Fridays, 4:00-5:30PM October 2 October 23 November 27

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This month's theme: Control Systems This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.
